meta.description: Construct and analyze protein-protein interaction networks by querying the STRING database for input gene lists. Inputs: DEG or candidate gene list. Outputs: PPI network graph, hub genes by degree/betweenness centrality, network topology statistics.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated biological or statistical claims were introduced across the audited outputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: Outputs remained technical and procedural; no diagnostic, prescriptive, or treatment advice was generated.

methodological_ground.detail: STRING-cache mapping, score filtering, graph construction, and plot-only reuse matched the documented workflow without methodological fallacies.

code_usability.detail: The CLI, bundled tests, and new regression checks all ran successfully after the fixes.

reliability.note: Failure modes are clear and recoverable; species validation still occurs after gene-list parsing, but no incorrect side effects remain.

agent_usability.note: The execution path is clear and example-driven, with only minor front-loaded logging before one validation failure path.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, layering, and escape hatches are strong; the skill remains slightly conservative in one validation order but behaves consistently.

inputs.label: Human bundled gene list
inputs.note: Executed cleanly and produced the full documented artifact set.

assertions.text: Output creates all documented artifacts
assertions.note: RDS, XLSX, CSV, PDF, and session info files were produced.

assertions.text: Summary metrics are internally consistent
assertions.note: Summary shows 8 input genes, 8 mapped genes, 7 nodes, 24 edges, and threshold 700.

assertions.text: Execution stays within the offline STRING workflow
assertions.note: The run used the bundled local cache and did not depend on remote services.

assertions.text: Output remains inside the requested skill-local directory
assertions.note: All artifacts were written under tests/output/audit-canonical.

assertions.text: Reproducibility controls are available for layout generation
assertions.note: The command accepted a fixed seed and completed deterministically for the data outputs.

inputs.label: Numeric species with styled plot
inputs.note: Numeric species normalization and supported styling options worked as documented.

assertions.text: Numeric species 9606 resolves successfully
assertions.note: The run completed without species normalization errors.

assertions.text: Supported style options do not break the run
assertions.note: Circle layout, curved edges, square nodes, and title rendering all succeeded.

assertions.text: The skill still produces the documented output set
assertions.note: The variant run wrote the same artifact contract as the canonical run.

assertions.text: Core network results stay consistent for the same input and threshold
assertions.note: The summary remained 8 mapped genes, 7 nodes, and 24 edges at threshold 700.

assertions.text: Execution stays in scope for a local STRING workflow
assertions.note: No unsupported data source or online API path was introduced.

inputs.label: Lower-bound threshold 400
inputs.note: The documented lower boundary was accepted and produced a denser network with a useful warning.

assertions.text: Threshold lower bound 400 is accepted
assertions.note: The CLI accepted the documented boundary value.

assertions.text: The tool emits a useful low-threshold warning
assertions.note: A warning explained that values below 700 are lower-confidence.

assertions.text: Output files are still produced successfully
assertions.note: The analysis completed and wrote the expected artifacts.

assertions.text: Summary metrics reflect the changed threshold
assertions.note: The summary records threshold 400 and 28 retained edges.

assertions.text: The workflow remains methodologically coherent at the boundary
assertions.note: Lowering the threshold changed network density without breaking graph construction.

inputs.label: Plot-only regeneration
inputs.note: Plot-only reuse behaved as documented and avoided unnecessary rebuild work.

assertions.text: Plot-only mode succeeds from a prior full run
assertions.note: The command regenerated the plot without a new gene-list analysis.

assertions.text: The CLI does not force unnecessary rebuild work
assertions.note: The log skipped gene-list reading and bundle reconstruction.

assertions.text: Output stays in the existing target directory
assertions.note: The regenerated PDF remained under tests/output/audit-canonical/plot.

assertions.text: The reuse path matches the documented contract
assertions.note: The run depended on an existing data/ppi_result.rds bundle as documented.

inputs.label: High-option styled run
inputs.note: A broad supported option set executed successfully without destabilizing the analysis outputs.

assertions.text: A broad supported option set executes successfully
assertions.note: The extended styling, mapping, and figure options were accepted without errors.

assertions.text: Data outputs remain stable for the same input and threshold
assertions.note: The summary still reports 8 mapped genes, 7 nodes, and 24 edges.

assertions.text: Plot customization stays within the documented feature set
assertions.note: FR layout, dotted edges, mapped aesthetics, and custom colors all completed successfully.

assertions.text: Reproducibility and containment remain intact
assertions.note: The run accepted a fixed seed and wrote only inside the requested output directory.

assertions.text: The full output contract is preserved under higher option complexity
assertions.note: The standard RDS, XLSX, CSV, PDF, and session info files were produced.

inputs.label: Unsupported species rejection
inputs.note: The command failed on the expected validation path and produced no fresh output directory.

assertions.text: Unsupported species is rejected with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER
assertions.note: The command exited on the documented validation path.

assertions.text: The error message tells the user how to recover
assertions.note: The message listed the allowed species values directly.

assertions.text: A fresh failing run does not create a new output directory
assertions.note: tests/output/audit-invalid-species-fresh did not exist after execution.

assertions.text: The command fails before any cache or file-output side effects
assertions.note: No output tree or analysis artifacts were created.

inputs.label: Invalid line_type rejection
inputs.note: The unsupported line_type value now fails validation immediately with no side effects.

assertions.text: Unsupported line_type values are rejected with SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER
assertions.note: The command now fails validation instead of silently coercing the value.

assertions.text: Runtime behavior now matches the documented contract for unsupported plot options
assertions.note: The CLI error is consistent with SKILL.md and troubleshooting.md.

assertions.text: A fresh failing run does not create a new output directory
assertions.note: tests/output/audit-invalid-line-type-fresh did not exist after execution.

assertions.text: Validation fails before any analysis side effects
assertions.note: No cache processing or output artifacts were produced.

key_strengths: The skill is genuinely runnable offline with a bundled STRING cache and reproducible seed controls.
The R implementation is modular, and the packaged smoke tests plus new regression checks passed without modification.
Input validation, path containment, and documented failure modes now align with runtime behavior.
Successful runs consistently wrote the documented bundle, tables, PDF plot, and session information.

recommendations.title: Maintain regression coverage
recommendations.problem: No blocking issue was identified, but continued regression coverage is needed before public distribution.
recommendations.root_cause: Data Analysis skills depend on local package and data assumptions that can drift.
recommendations.fix: Keep smoke tests and example inputs synchronized with each release.
